claim: Hermes remembers conversations across sessions using external memory storage with full-text search via SQLite FTS5, enabling context recall without re-uploading files
actionable: When building agents, use external memory systems with FTS5 for persistent session recall to reduce token usage and improve user experience
source_title: Hermes: The Self-Improving Agent That Gets Smarter Every Day

claim: Hermes compresses session context at 50% threshold rather than 80%, removing old tool outputs but preserving conversation head and tail
actionable: When implementing context compression for agents, experiment with different compression thresholds (50-80%) and selective preservation of key parts
source_title: Hermes: The Self-Improving Agent That Gets Smarter Every Day

claim: Hermes automatically creates reusable skills from experience every 10 turns by analyzing conversation patterns and extracting procedures
actionable: Build agents that introspectively extract their own procedures into tools; trigger this every 10 turns to reduce repetition overhead
source_title: Hermes: The Self-Improving Agent That Gets Smarter Every Day

claim: Memory in Hermes is preloaded as a compacted ~3.5k character version (roughly 700 tokens) to keep token usage low across sessions
actionable: Use compacted memory summaries (3.5k chars) rather than full conversation history to reduce context window strain while preserving essential information
source_title: Hermes: The Self-Improving Agent That Gets Smarter Every Day
